When tracking a player, it spits a stream of Ichor at them, similar to that of the {{Golden Shower}}.

The stream inflicts the {{Ichor debuff}} for 15 / {{||30||}} / {{|||37.5|||}} seconds, lowering the player's defense by 15.

Upon death, Ichor Stickers will also drop {{Ichor}}.

icy_merman.name: Icy Merman
icy_merman.tooltip:
The Icy Merman is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Ice biome}}.

Upon gaining line of sight of the player, it will stop in place and begin firing Icewater Spit projectiles similar to those fired by the {{Frost Staff}}, but with less frequency.

Its projectiles also behave similarly to those of the {{Ice Elemental}}, but deal less damage and are fired more rapidly.

The Icewater Spit attack always inflicts the {{Chilled}} {{debuff}}, and has a 1/10 (10%) chance to inflict {{Frozen}}.

illuminant_bat.name: Illuminant Bat
illuminant_bat.tooltip:
The Illuminant Bat is a {{Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} that only spawns in the {{Underground Hallow}}.

It is the {{Hallowed}} version of the {{Giant Bat}}, but is much stronger and emits a blurred, after-image effect.

illuminant_slime.name: Illuminant Slime
illuminant_slime.tooltip:
The Illuminant Slime is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that spawns in the {{Underground Hallow}}, replacing normal {{cave}} slimes ({{Black}} and {{Mother Slimes}}) in most cases.

Compared to these cave counterparts, it deals much more damage and has more health.

The Illuminant Slime gives off a small amount of light and leaves an after-image (like the {{Chaos Elemental}} and {{Illuminant Bat}}), which appears as a long, pink trail.

blood_jelly.name: Blood Jelly
blood_jelly.tooltip:
Jellyfish are {{enemies}} found in {{water}}.

When underwater, they attack by propelling themselves towards the player in short bursts.

When outside of water, they will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

Jellyfish are able to jump out of the water to attack players, often leaving them stranded on land.

Most varieties of Jellyfish produce light, making them easy to spot in dark areas.

There are currently 5 Jellyfish variants, 3 of which only appear during {{Hardmode}}.

blue_jellyfish.name: Blue Jellyfish
blue_jellyfish.tooltip:
Jellyfish are {{enemies}} found in {{water}}.

When underwater, they attack by propelling themselves towards the player in short bursts.

When outside of water, they will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

Jellyfish are able to jump out of the water to attack players, often leaving them stranded on land.

Most varieties of Jellyfish produce light, making them easy to spot in dark areas.

There are currently 5 Jellyfish variants, 3 of which only appear during {{Hardmode}}.

fungo_fish.name: Fungo Fish
fungo_fish.tooltip:
Jellyfish are {{enemies}} found in {{water}}.

When underwater, they attack by propelling themselves towards the player in short bursts.

When outside of water, they will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

Jellyfish are able to jump out of the water to attack players, often leaving them stranded on land.

Most varieties of Jellyfish produce light, making them easy to spot in dark areas.

There are currently 5 Jellyfish variants, 3 of which only appear during {{Hardmode}}.

green_jellyfish.name: Green Jellyfish
green_jellyfish.tooltip:
Jellyfish are {{enemies}} found in {{water}}.

When underwater, they attack by propelling themselves towards the player in short bursts.

When outside of water, they will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

Jellyfish are able to jump out of the water to attack players, often leaving them stranded on land.

Most varieties of Jellyfish produce light, making them easy to spot in dark areas.

There are currently 5 Jellyfish variants, 3 of which only appear during {{Hardmode}}.

pink_jellyfish.name: Pink Jellyfish
pink_jellyfish.tooltip:
Jellyfish are {{enemies}} found in {{water}}.

When underwater, they attack by propelling themselves towards the player in short bursts.

When outside of water, they will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

Jellyfish are able to jump out of the water to attack players, often leaving them stranded on land.

Most varieties of Jellyfish produce light, making them easy to spot in dark areas.

There are currently 5 Jellyfish variants, 3 of which only appear during {{Hardmode}}.

jungle_bat.name: Jungle Bat
jungle_bat.tooltip:
The Jungle Bat is a common {{bat}} {{enemy}} found only in the {{Jungle}} and less commonly in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

In {{Expert Mode}}, it can inflict the {{Feral Bite}} {{debuff}}.

Like most other bats, it cannot enter {{water}}.

jungle_creeper.name: Jungle Creeper
jungle_creeper.tooltip:
The Jungle Creeper is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

It resembles a {{Black Recluse}} colored brown with a bit of green, and climbs on {{background walls}} similar to the {{Wall Creeper}}.

jungle_slime.name: Jungle Slime
jungle_slime.tooltip:
The Jungle Slime is a type of {{slime}} only found in the {{Jungle}} during the daytime.

They have relatively high damage and health compared to most {{Pre-Hardmode}} slimes of their size.

kobold.name: Kobold
kobold.tooltip:
The Kobold is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 2 during waves 2–7, and Tier 3 during waves 2–5.

kobold_glider.name: Kobold Glider
kobold_glider.tooltip:
The Kobold Glider is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 2 during waves 5–7, and Tier 3 during waves 4–5.

krampus.name: Krampus
krampus.tooltip: The Krampus is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 8 during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

lac_beetle.name: Lac Beetle
lac_beetle.tooltip:
The Lac Beetle is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Jungle}} biome.

When killed, it will drop the {{Violet Husk}}, which can be used to make {{Violet Dye}}.

lamia.name: Lamia
lamia.tooltip:
The Lamia is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that can only be found in the {{Underground Desert}} {{biome}}.

When found in regular or {{Hallowed}} Underground Deserts, it has pale blonde hair and has a chance to drop the {{Sun Mask}}.

{{Corruption}}/{{Crimson}} Underground Deserts will spawn an alternate variant having much darker hair than its normal counterpart.

The "corrupted" version has a chance to drop the {{Moon Mask}}.

lava_bat.name: Lava Bat
lava_bat.tooltip:
The Lava Bat is a {{Hardmode}} {{bat}} {{enemy}} that appears in the {{The Underworld}} after one of the {{mechanical bosses}} has been defeated.

They are a stronger version of {{Hellbats}}.

lava_slime.name: Lava Slime
lava_slime.tooltip:
The Lava Slime is a {{pre-Hardmode}} enemy and a slime variant exclusive to {{The Underworld}}.

 It gives off an orange glow with fire particles.

Lava Slimes take no damage from lava and float on it like regular slimes do on water, except they rebound very high into the air when they come up, instead of gently bouncing up and down like normal slimes.

diseaster_bunny.name: Diseaster Bunny
diseaster_bunny.tooltip:
Diseaster Bunnies come from the small eggs laid by {{Lepus}}, and therefore look like a miniature version of it.

They also appear instead of {{Bunnies}} during {{Easter}}, at any time of the day.

It acts exactly like a {{Corrupt Bunny}}.

They can be quite annoying for new players, due to their high attack, health, speed and jumping ability, and the fact that they can spawn anywhere on the surface at any time.

lihzahrd.name: Lihzahrd
lihzahrd.tooltip:
The Lihzahrd is an {{enemy}} found only inside the {{Jungle Temple}} that initially behaves like an ordinary walking {{fighter}}.

However, as soon as its health is reduced to below 55% (220 / {{||440||}} / {{|||660|||}}), it enters its second, crawling form, which consists of a more lizard-like quadrupedal creature.

In this form, the Lihzahrd is much faster, has additional damage and defense, and is immune to {{knockback}}, but has reduced jump height.

maggot_zombie.name: Maggot Zombie
maggot_zombie.tooltip:
The Maggot Zombie is a {{Zombie}} variant that spawns in the {{Graveyard}} {{mini-biome}}.

When a Maggot Zombie is killed, up to three {{Maggots}} will be spawned.

These zombies have a more dreadful and rotten appearance than other Zombies, and the sprite also shows that they may be missing an eye.

man_eater.name: Man Eater
man_eater.tooltip:
The Man Eater is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Jungle}} {{biome}}, and is nearly identical in appearance and behavior to the above-ground {{Snatcher}}.

Man Eaters spawn anchored to blocks, with an extendable stem topped by a pincher.

The stem itself is a passive component and can be walked through safely without incurring damage; only the pincher inflicts or takes damage.

martian_drone.name: Martian Drone
martian_drone.tooltip:
The Martian Drone is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It can move through solid {{blocks}}.

When it is positioned lower than the player, it will directly fly towards a point just above the player.

After reaching the point, or the player is positioned lower than the Martian Drone, it will dive down towards the player.

Once it gets close enough to the player, it self-destructs, dealing area-of-effect damage.

martian_engineer.name: Martian Engineer
martian_engineer.tooltip:
The Martian Engineer is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that appears during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

Martian Engineers have the ability to build {{Tesla Turrets}} around them.

bubble_shield.name: Bubble Shield
bubble_shield.tooltip:
The Martian Officer is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It will always initially spawn with a protective Bubble Shield, which provides the Officer significantly added defense and hit points, as well as immunity to {{lava}} and {{knockback}}, until it is depleted.

After the Bubble Shield is destroyed, the Officer can now actually be harmed.

If the Martian Officer is not killed within three seconds after the Bubble Shield is depleted, the Bubble Shield will re-activate with full strength.

The Officer's health will not be restored.

martian_saucer_cannon.name: Martian Saucer Cannon
martian_saucer_cannon.tooltip:
The Martian Saucer is a large flying {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{mini-boss}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It can only spawn if the Martian Madness event is 30% completed or higher, and it can move through {{blocks}}.

It has four turrets with independent health meters that each must be destroyed, which include two rocket launchers and two laser machine guns.

In addition, the Martian Saucer itself fires a "Martian Deathray", a large yellow beam that can only be fired directly downward and cannot be destroyed independently.

martian_saucer_turret.name: Martian Saucer Turret
martian_saucer_turret.tooltip:
The Martian Saucer is a large flying {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{mini-boss}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It can only spawn if the Martian Madness event is 30% completed or higher, and it can move through {{blocks}}.

It has four turrets with independent health meters that each must be destroyed, which include two rocket launchers and two laser machine guns.

In addition, the Martian Saucer itself fires a "Martian Deathray", a large yellow beam that can only be fired directly downward and cannot be destroyed independently.

martian_walker.name: Martian Walker
martian_walker.tooltip:
The Martian Walker is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It appears as a large tripod enemy that moves at a very fast pace, and has the ability to fire blue Laser Rays at the player, with a maximum fire rate of once every 0.12 seconds (7 ticks) or 8.6 shots per second.

These traits combined with its amount of {{health}} and its resistance against {{knockback}} can make this enemy somewhat difficult to defeat.

mc_moneypants.name: Mc MoneyPants
mc_moneypants.tooltip:
Mc MoneyPants was an unspawnable {{NPC}} introduced alongside the {{Chinese New Year}} content.

It served no unique purpose, and was promptly removed.

bound_mechanic.name: Bound Mechanic
bound_mechanic.tooltip: The Mechanic is an {{NPC}} vendor who sells {{wire}}, {{actuators}}, and various {{mechanisms}}, as well as {{Wire Cutters}} and {{Wrenches}}, allowing the player to construct and activate wired devices like {{traps}} and {{critter engines}}.

medusa.name: Medusa
medusa.tooltip:
Medusa is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found near {{Marble Caves}}.

She is a relatively fast-moving melee attacker who has the unique ability to inflict the {{Stoned}} {{debuff}} through her gaze.

This debuff deals 20 / {{||40||}} / {{|||60|||}} damage to the player and turns the player to stone:

meteor_head.name: Meteor Head
meteor_head.tooltip:
The Meteor Head is a flying {{enemy}} that only spawns in a {{Meteorite}} {{biome}}.

They override all other enemy spawns in the biome, except when the Meteorite biome is within the {{Ocean}}.

Once only 74 blocks of Meteorite remain, the area is no longer considered a Meteorite biome.

Therefore, Meteor Heads will no longer spawn.

milkyway_weaver.name: Milkyway Weaver
milkyway_weaver.tooltip:
The Milkyway Weaver is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}}, {{worm}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Stardust Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It is similar to the {{Solar Pillar}}'s {{Crawltipede}}, in that it is completely unaffected by gravity and floats freely through the air.

However, it is much smaller than the Crawltipede, targets players anywhere instead of only in the air, and rather than its tail, its head is its only vulnerable part.

corrupt_mimic.name: Corrupt Mimic
corrupt_mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

crimson_mimic.name: Crimson Mimic
crimson_mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

hallowed_mimic.name: Hallowed Mimic
hallowed_mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

ice_mimic.name: Ice Mimic
ice_mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

jungle_mimic.name: Jungle Mimic
jungle_mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

mimic.name: Mimic
mimic.tooltip: Mimics are rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} disguised as {{chests}}, appearing as the appropriate chest type for the environment.

mister_stabby.name: Mister Stabby
mister_stabby.tooltip:
Mister Stabby is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawns during the {{Frost Legion}} {{event}}.

It chases the player and occasionally stops in place to do a considerably long stabbing animation with its knife.

When attacking the player, it will dash and bounce around the terrain, making it difficult to hit.

moss_hornet.name: Moss Hornet
moss_hornet.tooltip:
The Moss Hornet is a {{Hardmode}} flying {{enemy}} similar to the {{pre-Hardmode}} {{Hornet}}, being encountered in the {{Underground Jungle}} biome and requiring blocks of {{Jungle Grass}} in order to spawn.

Compared to the more random flying patterns of pre-Hardmode Hornets, Moss Hornets act more aggressively, hovering closer to the player's position and shooting their stingers in rapid bursts.

The stingers have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}} to the player for 10 seconds.

In {{Expert Mode}}, the chance is 1/3 (33.33%) for 20 seconds and 2/3 (66.67%) for 2–10 seconds.

They cannot be destroyed with weapons and travel at about the same speed as the darts from a {{Dart Trap}}.

moth.name: Moth
moth.tooltip:
The Moth is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Jungle}}.

It flies around very slowly, and upon seeing the player, will direct its attention towards them and attempt to deal contact damage to them.

Upon killing the Moth, there is a chance that it will drop {{Butterfly Dust}}, which is solely used to craft {{Butterfly Wings}}.

The Butterfly Dust can only drop once any {{mechanical boss}} has been defeated in the current world.

mother_slime.name: Mother Slime
mother_slime.tooltip:
The Mother Slime is a type of {{slime}} that is found in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It spawns 1-3 {{Baby Slimes}} upon death, and like all slimes, drops {{Gel}}.

It is one of the largest ordinary slimes in the game, only topped by {{King Slime}}, {{Queen Slime}}, and the {{Rainbow Slime}}.

baby_mothron.name: Baby Mothron
baby_mothron.tooltip:
Mothron is a powerful {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

Mothrons can pass through all {{blocks}} and attack by chasing after the player or aligning themselves horizontally with them before lunging towards them at varying speeds, inflicting heavy contact damage depending on their velocity.

Occasionally, it lands on blocks to lay several Mothron Eggs that will spawn Baby Mothrons if they are not destroyed in time.

mothron_egg.name: Mothron Egg
mothron_egg.tooltip:
Mothron is a powerful {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

Mothrons can pass through all {{blocks}} and attack by chasing after the player or aligning themselves horizontally with them before lunging towards them at varying speeds, inflicting heavy contact damage depending on their velocity.

Occasionally, it lands on blocks to lay several Mothron Eggs that will spawn Baby Mothrons if they are not destroyed in time.

mothron.name: Mothron
mothron.tooltip:
Mothron is a powerful {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

Mothrons can pass through all {{blocks}} and attack by chasing after the player or aligning themselves horizontally with them before lunging towards them at varying speeds, inflicting heavy contact damage depending on their velocity.

Occasionally, it lands on blocks to lay several Mothron Eggs that will spawn Baby Mothrons if they are not destroyed in time.

blood_mummy.name: Blood Mummy
blood_mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

dark_mummy.name: Dark Mummy
dark_mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

light_mummy.name: Light Mummy
light_mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

mummy.name: Mummy
mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

shadow_mummy.name: Shadow Mummy
shadow_mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

spectral_mummy.name: Spectral Mummy
spectral_mummy.tooltip:
Mummies are {{Hardmode}} {{Desert}} {{enemies}}.

They spawn in areas where {{Sand Blocks}} are present and follow the {{Fighter AI}} (behaving similar to {{Zombies}}).

mushi_ladybug.name: Mushi Ladybug
mushi_ladybug.tooltip:
The Mushi Ladybug is an {{enemy}} that spawns in {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}}, both on the surface and below-ground in {{Hardmode}}, but only on the surface in {{pre-Hardmode}}.

The Mushi Ladybug's high health and damage, quick movement, and ability to fly short distances place them among the more dangerous enemies that can spawn before Hardmode.

mythical_wyvern_head.name: Mythical Wyvern Head
mythical_wyvern_head.tooltip:
The Mythical Wyvern is a removed, unimplemented flying {{enemy}} that was associated with the {{Chinese New Year}} {{seasonal event}}.

It was a variant of the {{Wyvern}}.

nailhead.name: Nailhead
nailhead.tooltip:
The Nailhead is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} found only during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

In addition to dealing high contact damage, the Nailhead has a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to fire three to five Nail projectiles in a wide spread when attacked.

He has significantly more health than most other Solar Eclipse enemies and has a high resistance to {{knockback}}.

nebula_floater.name: Nebula Floater
nebula_floater.tooltip:
The Nebula Floater is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} flying {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Nebula Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

The Nebula Floater periodically spawns up to three Nebula Eyes that float around it.

If it has a clear line of sight to the player, it will hover in place and start firing pink Nebula Lasers from one of the Nebula Eyes at the player, otherwise, it will move towards the player through all tiles.

It can also do random teleports around the player periodically, and will also attempt to do so with a small chance if it is hit.

necromancer.name: Necromancer
necromancer.tooltip:
The Necromancer is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It attacks by rapidly firing Shadow Beams at the player, thin rays of purple light that reflect off walls multiple times.

They travel at an extremely high velocity, being fired nearly instantly, and inflict the {{Slow}} {{debuff}}.

Necromancers teleport to a different location on-screen after firing projectiles six times or when being hit.

nutcracker.name: Nutcracker
nutcracker.tooltip:
The Nutcracker is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 3 during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

There are two Nutcracker variants, each with slightly differing {{damage}} and {{defense}}.

The first variant will transform into the more powerful second variant when its health is reduced to below 55% (990 / {{||1,485||}} / {{|||2,227|||}}).

The Nutcracker's spinning second form gains a higher resistance to {{knockback}} and significantly increased movement speed.

lost_girl.name: Lost Girl
lost_girl.tooltip:
The Nymph is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Cavern}} layer of any {{biome}}.

When encountered, she appears as the Lost Girl, who seems to be a helpless, stationary {{NPC}} in need of rescue.

However, when a player gets close or attacks the Lost Girl, she transforms into the Nymph and starts attacking the player.

For a {{pre-Hardmode}} enemy, she deals comparatively high damage.

nymph.name: Nymph
nymph.tooltip:
The Nymph is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Cavern}} layer of any {{biome}}.

When encountered, she appears as the Lost Girl, who seems to be a helpless, stationary {{NPC}} in need of rescue.

However, when a player gets close or attacks the Lost Girl, she transforms into the Nymph and starts attacking the player.

For a {{pre-Hardmode}} enemy, she deals comparatively high damage.

old_ones_skeleton.name: Old One's Skeleton
old_ones_skeleton.tooltip: The Old One's Skeleton is an {{enemy}} which is spawned by the {{Dark Mage}} during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

paladin.name: Paladin
paladin.tooltip:
The Paladin is a rare, resilient {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It is immune to {{knockback}} and attacks by rapidly throwing hammer projectiles which can pass through {{blocks}} and inflict extreme damage.

parrot.name: Parrot
parrot.tooltip:
The Parrot is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that is found during a {{Pirate Invasion}}.

It flies at the player, trying to deal contact damage.

It will occasionally roost on any {{block}} if not pursuing the player.

pigron.name: Pigron
pigron.tooltip:
The Pigron is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found in the {{Ice biome}} when it is overlapped with the {{Underground Hallow}}, {{Underground Corruption}}, or {{Underground Crimson}}.

It comes in multiple colors, depending on which biome it spawns in, but all share the same statistics.

pinky.name: Pinky
pinky.tooltip:
Pinky is a rare {{slime}} which spawns in place of certain types of other slimes.

Despite its small size, Pinky has more {{health}} and {{defense}} than nearly all other {{pre-Hardmode}} slimes, though it has one of the lowest values of damage and {{knockback}} resistance in the game.

It is also the only source of {{Pink Gel}}.

piranha.name: Piranha
piranha.tooltip:
The Piranha is an {{enemy}} that can be found in {{water}} pools in the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}} biomes and in the {{Cavern}} layer.

It is twice as common in the Jungle biomes.

pirate_captain.name: Pirate Captain
pirate_captain.tooltip:
The Pirate Captain is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which appears during the {{Pirate Invasion}} {{event}}.

Upon being within line of sight of the player, he attacks using an assault rifle-like gun that shoots at a speed comparable to the {{Minishark}}, then stops to fire a single cannonball from its underbarrel mounted cannon while stationary.

The cannonball has a short range and detonates upon impact with {{blocks}}, much like those fired from the {{Flying Dutchman}}, and deals immense amounts of damage.

pirate_corsair.name: Pirate Corsair
pirate_corsair.tooltip:
The Pirate Corsair is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns commonly during the {{Pirate Invasion}}.

It acts similarly to the {{Pirate Deckhand}}, but has slightly higher stats.

pirate_crossbower.name: Pirate Crossbower
pirate_crossbower.tooltip:
The Pirate Crossbower is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} that spawns during the {{Pirate Invasion}} {{event}}.

She attacks more slowly than the {{Pirate Deadeye}} but fires much more dangerous projectiles at the player in the form of {{Flaming Arrows}}.

The Flaming Arrows have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance of inflicting the {{On Fire!}} {{debuff}} for 7 / {{||14||}} / {{|||17.5|||}} seconds.

pirate_deadeye.name: Pirate Deadeye
pirate_deadeye.tooltip:
The Pirate Deadeye is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Pirate Invasion}} {{event}}.

It appears similar to the {{Pirate Deckhand}} but comes armed with a blunderbuss that can fire bullets at the player, an eye patch, and slightly modified stats.

pirate_deckhand.name: Pirate Deckhand
pirate_deckhand.tooltip:
The Pirate Deckhand is a {{Hardmode}} enemy which appears during a {{Pirate Invasion}}.

It is a fairly basic enemy which will try running and jumping at the player to inflict damage.

pixie.name: Pixie
pixie.tooltip:
The Pixie is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns frequently in the {{Hallow}} at the {{surface}} layer.

Pixies hover a limited height aboveground, emitting a fair amount of flickering light, and can inflict the {{Slow}} or {{Silenced}} {{debuffs}}.

They always drop {{Pixie Dust}}, a common Hardmode {{crafting material}}.

planteras_hook.name: Plantera's Hook
planteras_hook.tooltip:
Plantera is a {{Hardmode}} {{boss}} that can be fought in the {{Underground Jungle}} after all three {{mechanical bosses}} ({{The Twins}}, {{The Destroyer}}, and {{Skeletron Prime}}) have been defeated.

Plantera drops a {{Temple Key}} upon being defeated, allowing access to the {{Jungle Temple}}.

planteras_tentacle.name: Plantera's Tentacle
planteras_tentacle.tooltip:
Plantera is a {{Hardmode}} {{boss}} that can be fought in the {{Underground Jungle}} after all three {{mechanical bosses}} ({{The Twins}}, {{The Destroyer}}, and {{Skeletron Prime}}) have been defeated.

Plantera drops a {{Temple Key}} upon being defeated, allowing access to the {{Jungle Temple}}.

spore.name: Spore
spore.tooltip:
Plantera is a {{Hardmode}} {{boss}} that can be fought in the {{Underground Jungle}} after all three {{mechanical bosses}} ({{The Twins}}, {{The Destroyer}}, and {{Skeletron Prime}}) have been defeated.

Plantera drops a {{Temple Key}} upon being defeated, allowing access to the {{Jungle Temple}}.

poltergeist.name: Poltergeist
poltergeist.tooltip:
The Poltergeist is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 4 of the {{Pumpkin Moon}} {{event}}.

It behaves similarly to a {{Wraith}}, except it can fly freely through the air without needing solid ground to hover on.

possessed_armor.name: Possessed Armor
possessed_armor.tooltip:
The Possessed Armor is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which spawns on the {{surface}} at {{night}}, and {{underground}} at any time.

It walks quickly and casts a motion blur similar to that of the {{Shadow armor}}.

It can always open doors, regardless of whether or not there is a blood moon.

predictor.name: Predictor
predictor.tooltip:
The Predictor is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Nebula Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

Its main attack is firing five Nebula piercer projectiles at the player, which travel in a straight line in a tight spread.

present_mimic.name: Present Mimic
present_mimic.tooltip:
The Present Mimic is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

It acts like normal {{Mimics}} do, but passes itself off as a present instead of a {{Chest}}.

Present Mimics may spawn at any wave, instead of wave-appropriate enemies, if there are currently less than four Present Mimics around.

psycho.name: Psycho
psycho.tooltip:
The Psycho is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} {{event}}.

The Psycho will initially spawn in a nearly invisible, stealthed state, waiting for the player to damage him or draw near before he fully reveals himself and starts attacking.

An attack made by contact with the Psycho while in stealth will deal significantly more damage than an ordinary hit, similar to the function of the {{Psycho Knife}} it drops.

purple_slime.name: Purple Slime
purple_slime.tooltip:
The Purple Slime is a type of {{slime}} found in {{Forests}} during the day.

It is a larger and stronger variant of the {{Blue Slime}}.

It cannot spawn within 400 tiles of the world's natural spawn point, unless in {{Expert Mode}}.

Purple Slimes will not usually target the player, but will become aggressive when damaged, during the night, or if brought underground.

ragged_caster.name: Ragged Caster
ragged_caster.tooltip:
The Ragged Caster is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It attacks by firing three homing Lost Soul projectiles at the player which decelerate as they approach the player and inflict the {{Blackout}} {{debuff}} for 2–6 / {{||4–12||}} / {{|||5–15|||}} seconds on them.

Ragged Casters teleport to a different location on-screen after firing six rounds of projectiles or immediately upon being hit.

rainbow_slime.name: Rainbow Slime
rainbow_slime.tooltip:
The Rainbow Slime is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that spawns in the {{Hallow}} while it is {{raining}}.

It constantly changes color, as do the {{Rainbow Bricks}} it drops.

raincoat_zombie.name: Raincoat Zombie
raincoat_zombie.tooltip:
The Raincoat Zombie is a {{zombie}} variant that only appears during the {{rain}} on the {{surface}} at {{night}}.

It acts the same as normal zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage, while attempting to navigate around obstacles and only being able to open {{doors}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

It is differentiated from other zombies in that it is dressed in traditional yellow raincoat attire, which it can also drop in the form of the {{Rain Hat}} and {{Rain Coat}} {{armor}} pieces.

zombie.name: Zombie
zombie.tooltip:
The Zombie is a common {{pre-Hardmode}} {{surface}} {{enemy}} that appears at {{night}}, and is a primary reason newer players will want to build a shelter before night arrives.

Along with {{Demon Eyes}}, they are generally among the first nighttime enemies a player will encounter, as they appear in areas a player first {{spawns}}.

raven.name: Raven
raven.tooltip: The Raven is a hostile flying {{enemy}} that appears in {{Graveyards}} and can be found in the {{Forest}} at {{night}} during the {{Halloween}} {{seasonal event}}.

ray_gunner.name: Ray Gunner
ray_gunner.tooltip:
The Ray Gunner is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It fires red laser projectiles, at a maximum fire rate of once every 0.67 seconds (40 ticks).

reaper.name: Reaper
reaper.tooltip:
The Reaper is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during a {{Solar Eclipse}}.

It will only spawn after all {{mechanical bosses}} have been defeated.

red_devil.name: Red Devil
red_devil.tooltip:
The Red Devil is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in {{The Underworld}} after any {{mechanical boss}} has been defeated.

While flying toward the player in a similar manner to that of the {{Demon}}, it fires four purple trident projectiles that deal extremely large amounts of damage upon contact.

It is considered a more difficult version of the Demon, having more health and dealing much more damage.

Similar to the Demon, it drops a {{magic weapon}} resembling its means of attack, the {{Unholy Trident}}.

red_slime.name: Red Slime
red_slime.tooltip:
The Red Slime is a type of {{slime}} that is more powerful than {{Blue Slimes}}.

They appear exclusively in the {{Underground}} {{layer}}, and will not appear in the deeper {{Caverns}} layer.

rock_golem.name: Rock Golem
rock_golem.tooltip:
The Rock Golem is a powerful {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Cavern}} layer.

It throws rocks at players that are within its line of sight but out of its contact range.

When being attacked, it continues to attack, making stun-lock options unusable.

rune_wizard.name: Rune Wizard
rune_wizard.tooltip:
The Rune Wizard is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{caster}} {{enemy}} that is found in the lower {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

Upon spawning, he will proceed to fire magical Rune Blast projectiles at the player that penetrate terrain, similar to the {{Fire Imp}}'s {{Burning Spheres}}.

However, unlike Burning Spheres, the Rune Wizard's projectiles cannot be destroyed in any way.

He will teleport to a new location after firing six projectiles, and will always drop the {{Rune Hat}} and {{Rune Robe}} upon death.

rusty_armored_bones.name: Rusty Armored Bones
rusty_armored_bones.tooltip:
The Rusty Armored Bones is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

Similar to {{Angry Bones}}, they come in multiple varieties and follow the {{Fighter AI}}.

They have a 1/3 (33.33%) chance to inflict the {{Bleeding}} {{debuff}} for 10 / {{||20||}} / {{|||25|||}} seconds and a 2/9 (22.22%) chance to inflict the {{Slow}} {{debuff}} for 5 / {{||10||}} / {{|||12.5|||}} seconds.

Only one of the debuffs can be inflicted at a time.

salamander.name: Salamander
salamander.tooltip:
The Salamander is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

It has nine color variants that all share identical stats and behavior.

When a player is within their line of sight, Salamanders stay in place and fire green spit projectiles that inflict the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}}.

sand_elemental.name: Sand Elemental
sand_elemental.tooltip:
The Sand Elemental is a rare {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} which spawns in the {{Desert}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

The Sand Elemental attacks by summoning three Ancient Storms randomly around the general location of the player, which each lasts 5 seconds.

The distance between them varies, and they remain stationary for their lifespan.

The Sand Elemental cannot travel through {{blocks}} and flies in a non-linear pattern.

She attempts to move toward the player and travels faster as her health is depleted.

She has a guaranteed chance to drop a {{Forbidden Fragment}}, the main ingredient for the {{Forbidden armor}}, {{Spirit Flame}}, and {{Sandstorm in a Bottle}}.

sand_poacher.name: Sand Poacher
sand_poacher.tooltip:
The Sand Poacher is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Underground Desert}} {{biome}}.

Following the {{Spider AI}}, it will follow and attempt to deal contact damage to the player while also being capable of climbing onto background {{walls}}.

If the Sand Poacher is climbing on a wall and detects the player within its line of sight, its movement speed increases drastically, making escape more difficult for the player.

Upon contact, the Sand Poacher also inflicts the {{Acid Venom}} {{debuff}} for 4 seconds.

bone_biter.name: Bone Biter
bone_biter.tooltip:
Sand Sharks are {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} only found in {{Desert}} {{biomes}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

They are able to "swim" through all varieties of {{Sand}}, {{Hardened Sand}}, and {{Sandstone Blocks}}, but will collide normally against any other block.

When they draw near a player, they will lunge out of the sand to attack.

crystal_thresher.name: Crystal Thresher
crystal_thresher.tooltip:
Sand Sharks are {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} only found in {{Desert}} {{biomes}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

They are able to "swim" through all varieties of {{Sand}}, {{Hardened Sand}}, and {{Sandstone Blocks}}, but will collide normally against any other block.

When they draw near a player, they will lunge out of the sand to attack.

flesh_reaver.name: Flesh Reaver
flesh_reaver.tooltip:
Sand Sharks are {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} only found in {{Desert}} {{biomes}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

They are able to "swim" through all varieties of {{Sand}}, {{Hardened Sand}}, and {{Sandstone Blocks}}, but will collide normally against any other block.

When they draw near a player, they will lunge out of the sand to attack.

sand_shark.name: Sand Shark
sand_shark.tooltip:
Sand Sharks are {{Hardmode}} {{enemies}} only found in {{Desert}} {{biomes}} during a {{Sandstorm}}.

They are able to "swim" through all varieties of {{Sand}}, {{Hardened Sand}}, and {{Sandstone Blocks}}, but will collide normally against any other block.

When they draw near a player, they will lunge out of the sand to attack.

sand_slime.name: Sand Slime
sand_slime.tooltip:
The Sand Slime is a type of {{slime}} that appears in the {{Underground Desert}}.

It can be recognized by its sandy color and texture.

Sand Slimes can drop a {{Slime Staff}} at slightly higher odds than nearly all other slimes, excluding {{Pinky}}.

scarecrow.name: Scarecrow
scarecrow.tooltip:
The Scarecrow is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Pumpkin Moon}} {{event}}.

There are ten varieties of Scarecrow, five of which lack legs and can only move by jumping, while the other five follow the normal {{Fighter AI}}.

Scarecrows have a 1/10 (10%) chance to inflict the {{Weak}} {{debuff}} for 1 minute / {{||2 minutes||}} / {{|||2 minutes and 30 seconds|||}}.

scutlix_gunner.name: Scutlix Gunner
scutlix_gunner.tooltip:
The Scutlix is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It consists of the Scutlix creature, ridden by a Scutlix Gunner enemy, both of which have individual health meters and can be killed independently.

When one is killed, the other will continue to attack.

scutlix.name: Scutlix
scutlix.tooltip:
The Scutlix is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Golem}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It consists of the Scutlix creature, ridden by a Scutlix Gunner enemy, both of which have individual health meters and can be killed independently.

When one is killed, the other will continue to attack.

sea_snail.name: Sea Snail
sea_snail.tooltip:
The Sea Snail is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Ocean}}.

The Sea Snail's spawn rate is the lowest of all enemies in the Ocean, one-third that of {{Squids}}.

It slowly crawls along the seafloor while attempting to deal contact damage.

When killed, it drops {{Purple Mucus}}, which is used solely to craft {{Purple Dye}} at a {{Dye Vat}}.

selenian.name: Selenian
selenian.tooltip:
The Selenian is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It is a ground-based enemy that wields two flaming swords and possesses a spinning attack that causes it to quickly lunge forward while reflecting the player's {{projectiles}}.

It takes triple the damage from other player-inflicted sources while spinning.

Selenians can reflect {{bullets}}, {{arrows}}, and {{darts}}, but not {{rockets}}, and the projectiles of numerous other weapons.

shark.name: Shark
shark.tooltip:
The Shark is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Ocean}}.

Sharks are rarer than other Ocean enemies like {{Crabs}} or {{Pink Jellyfish}}, though they are still relatively common.

shimmer_slime.name: Shimmer Slime
shimmer_slime.tooltip:
The Shimmer Slime is a type of {{slime}} that cannot be found naturally, and is instead created when most varieties of slimes come into contact with {{Shimmer}}.

Shimmer Slimes have the ability to reflect projectiles, similarly to Shimmer itself.

For the complete list, see the {{list of reflected projectiles}}.

skeleton_archer.name: Skeleton Archer
skeleton_archer.tooltip:
The Skeleton Archer is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that sometimes spawns in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

They move fairly slow compared to other cave enemies.

When the player is in their line of sight, they will stop moving and shoot {{Flaming Arrows}} at the player, which have a chance of inflicting the {{On Fire!}} {{debuff}}.

skeleton_commando.name: Skeleton Commando
skeleton_commando.tooltip:
The Skeleton Commando is a rare {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It slowly approaches the player, and, upon getting within their line of sight, stops in place before firing {{Rockets}} that explode upon contact with the player or with solid blocks.

skeleton_sniper.name: Skeleton Sniper
skeleton_sniper.tooltip:
The Skeleton Sniper is a rare {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

It attacks from long range, slowly approaching the player.

As soon as it has a clear line of sight, it pauses briefly before firing a single shot.

This shot can be fired from offscreen, travels at an extremely high velocity, and deals enough damage to kill a player with 500 {{health}} in three / {{||two||}} / {{|||one|||}} hit(s).

prime_cannon.name: Prime Cannon
prime_cannon.tooltip:
Skeletron Prime is a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of {{Skeletron}}.

It consists of a head and four limbs, but, similarly to Skeletron, only the head needs to be defeated to defeat the boss.

prime_laser.name: Prime Laser
prime_laser.tooltip:
Skeletron Prime is a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of {{Skeletron}}.

It consists of a head and four limbs, but, similarly to Skeletron, only the head needs to be defeated to defeat the boss.

prime_saw.name: Prime Saw
prime_saw.tooltip:
Skeletron Prime is a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of {{Skeletron}}.

It consists of a head and four limbs, but, similarly to Skeletron, only the head needs to be defeated to defeat the boss.

prime_vice.name: Prime Vice
prime_vice.tooltip:
Skeletron Prime is a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of {{Skeletron}}.

It consists of a head and four limbs, but, similarly to Skeletron, only the head needs to be defeated to defeat the boss.

slimeling.name: Slimeling
slimeling.tooltip:
The Slimeling is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} that is spawned in groups of two to three upon the death of a {{Corrupt Slime}}.

It has a chance to inflict the {{Darkness}} {{debuff}}.

slime.name: Slime
slime.tooltip:
Slimes are the most common {{enemy}} type.

They are almost always the first enemy the {{player}} encounters in a new world.

They come in multiple sizes and colors, each with different health, defense, and damage.

Slimes generally follow the basic {{Slime AI}}: They hop in the direction of the player and rest a short amount of time afterwards.

When a slime is preparing to hop, it bobs up and down rapidly.

Slimes can float in {{water}} and move significantly faster while doing so, but only the {{Lava Slime}} can float in {{lava}}.

dragon_snatcher.name: Dragon Snatcher
dragon_snatcher.tooltip:
The Snatcher is an {{enemy}} found in the surface {{Jungle}}, and is nearly identical in appearance and behavior to the {{Underground Jungle}}'s {{Man Eater}}.

Snatchers spawn anchored to blocks, with an extendable stem topped by a pincher.

The stem itself is a passive component and can be walked through safely without incurring damage; only the pincher inflicts or takes damage.

snatcher.name: Snatcher
snatcher.tooltip:
The Snatcher is an {{enemy}} found in the surface {{Jungle}}, and is nearly identical in appearance and behavior to the {{Underground Jungle}}'s {{Man Eater}}.

Snatchers spawn anchored to blocks, with an extendable stem topped by a pincher.

The stem itself is a passive component and can be walked through safely without incurring damage; only the pincher inflicts or takes damage.

snow_balla.name: Snow Balla
snow_balla.tooltip:
The Snow Balla is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawns during the {{Frost Legion}} {{event}}.

It throws {{snowballs}} at the player that form {{Snow Blocks}} on impact with other {{blocks}}, similar to the attack of the {{Antlion}}.

The Snow Blocks can also spawn in the air if Snow Balla throws it into the sky.

If it falls into a pit, it is possible for it to trap itself.

By trapping the Snow Balla, one can create a renewable farm for Snow Blocks in a similar manner to the Antlion-based sand farm.

snow_flinx.name: Snow Flinx
snow_flinx.tooltip:
The Snow Flinx is an {{enemy}} that can be found in the {{Ice biome}}.

It is very susceptible to {{knockback}}, and when hit, will tumble over and fly backwards.

snowman_gangsta.name: Snowman Gangsta
snowman_gangsta.tooltip:
The Snowman Gangsta is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawns during the {{Frost Legion}} {{event}}.

It fires {{Musket Ball}}-like projectiles at a slow rate and can only shoot left or right.

spiked_ice_slime.name: Spiked Ice Slime
spiked_ice_slime.tooltip:
The Spiked Ice Slime is a type of {{slime}} encountered in the {{Ice biome}}.

The Spiked Ice Slime fires Ice Spikes at the player, and the Ice Spikes and the slime itself always inflict the {{Chilled}} {{debuff}}, and have a chance to inflict the {{Frozen}} debuff.

spiked_jungle_slime.name: Spiked Jungle Slime
spiked_jungle_slime.tooltip:
The Spiked Jungle Slime is a type of {{slime}} encountered in the {{Underground Jungle}} biome.

It can fire Jungle Spikes at the player which have a chance to inflict the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}}.

In {{Expert Mode}}, it can also fire five Jungle Spikes at once around itself in a short range.

splinterling.name: Splinterling
splinterling.tooltip:
The Splinterling is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 2 of the {{Pumpkin Moon}} {{event}}.

It is one of the only sources of {{Spooky Wood}}, the other being the {{Mourning Wood}}.

spore_bat.name: Spore Bat
spore_bat.tooltip:
The Spore Bat is a {{bat}} {{enemy}} that spawns in underground {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}}.

It has the same stats as a {{Cave Bat}}.

However, Spore Bats have a chance to drop a {{Shroomerang}}.

In {{Expert Mode}}, it can inflict the {{Feral Bite}} {{debuff}} for 30–90 seconds.

spore_skeleton.name: Spore Skeleton
spore_skeleton.tooltip:
The Spore Skeleton is an {{enemy}} that spawns in underground {{Glowing Mushroom biomes}}.

All of its stats and behaviors are equivalent to normal {{Skeletons}}.

spore_zombie.name: Spore Zombie
spore_zombie.tooltip:
The Spore Zombie is a {{zombie}} variant that only appears on {{Mushroom Grass}} above the {{Surface}} at any point in the game.

It acts the same as normal zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage, while attempting to navigate around obstacles.

However, it is differentiable by normal zombies due to its mushroom-covered appearance, spawning at any {{time of day}}, and generally more powerful stats.

Unlike normal zombies, it is always able to open {{doors}} regardless of whether or not it is a {{Blood Moon}}.

squid.name: Squid
squid.tooltip:
The Squid is a rare {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Ocean}}.

When underwater, it attacks by propelling itself towards the player in short bursts.

If led out of the water, it will be unable to move or pursue the player in any way.

When killed, it always drops {{Black Ink}}, which is used solely to craft {{Black Dye}} at a {{Dye Vat}}.

sroller.name: Sroller
sroller.tooltip:
The Sroller is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Solar Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It can curl into a ball and roll at the player, similarly to a {{Giant Tortoise}} or {{Ice Tortoise}}, potentially dealing a large amount of damage on contact.

mini_star_cell.name: Mini Star Cell
mini_star_cell.tooltip:
The Star Cell is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Stardust Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It floats slowly towards the player but can move at a faster rate if the player begins moving away from it.

Upon death, it splits into multiple Mini Star Cells.

If these Mini Star Cells are not dealt with, they will grow into a full-sized Star Cell, that can also split into more Mini Star Cells.

Mini Star Cells do not contribute to the kill count required to break the Stardust Pillar's shield, though after growing back, they do.

stargazer.name: Stargazer
stargazer.tooltip:
The Stargazer is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Stardust Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

It walks slowly, and will sometimes fire a thin Stardust Laser at the player.

The Stargazer may move while firing the laser, but it cannot change the angle.

storm_diver.name: Storm Diver
storm_diver.tooltip:
The Storm Diver is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Vortex Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

Storm Divers wield a type of {{ranged weapon}} that is able to fire tight spreads of projectiles, similar to the {{Tactical Shotgun}}.

Storm Divers are also able to glide and hover with what appear to be {{jetpacks}}.

They appear to have mud-colored skin, a green glass helmet, cyan-colored claws, cyan lights, and an alien-like shotgun.

webbed_stylist.name: Webbed Stylist
webbed_stylist.tooltip:
The Stylist is an {{NPC}} vendor who can change the player's {{hairstyle}} and hair color.

She also sells a variety of {{Hair Dyes}}, which apply dynamic effects to the player's hair.

swamp_thing.name: Swamp Thing
swamp_thing.tooltip:
The Swamp Thing is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns only during a {{Solar Eclipse}}, during which he is about as common as a {{Zombie}}.

It moves very fast and occasionally stops in place, leaving it vulnerable to attacks.

tactical_skeleton.name: Tactical Skeleton
tactical_skeleton.tooltip:
The Tactical Skeleton is a rare {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Dungeon}} after Plantera is defeated.

Once the player is within its line of sight, the Tactical Skeleton remains stationary before firing volleys of four bullets at them that deal immense damage.

unconscious_man.name: Unconscious Man
unconscious_man.tooltip: The Tavernkeep is a unique {{NPC}} vendor who only accepts {{Defender Medals}} as currency for most items instead of {{coins}}.

tesla_turret.name: Tesla Turret
tesla_turret.tooltip:
The Tesla Turret is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} found only during the {{Martian Madness}} {{event}}.

It is built by {{Martian Engineers}} and is completely immobile.

Every three seconds, it will fire an Electric Bolt at the player, which has a 2/3 (66.67%) chance to inflict the {{Electrified}} {{debuff}} for 5 seconds.

The Tesla Turret will continue to stay in place until it is destroyed by the player.

the_bride.name: The Bride
the_bride.tooltip:
The Bride is a {{zombie}} variant that appears during a {{Blood Moon}} or very rarely at any time in a {{Graveyard}} biome.

She acts the same as normal zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage while attempting to navigate around obstacles and opening {{doors}} if they are in the way.

She has greater health than most other zombies and is dressed in a traditional bride's outfit, which she can drop in the form of the {{Wedding Veil}} and {{Wedding Dress}}.

the_groom.name: The Groom
the_groom.tooltip:
The Groom is a {{zombie}} variant that appears during a {{Blood Moon}} or very rarely at any time in a {{Graveyard}} biome.

He acts the same as normal Zombies, slowly walking towards the player to deal contact damage, while attempting to navigate around obstacles and opening {{doors}} if they are in the way.

He has greater health than most other Zombies, and is dressed in a tuxedo and top hat.

the_possessed.name: The Possessed
the_possessed.tooltip:
The Possessed is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Solar Eclipse}} event.

Despite not resembling one, she acts similarly to spider enemies such as {{Wall Creepers}}, {{Blood Crawlers}}, {{Black Recluses}}, and {{Jungle Creepers}}, climbing on {{background walls}} when able to and walking on the ground at all other times.

While climbing on walls, The Possessed seems to have an increased movement speed, similar to the other enemies that share this wall-climbing trait, and gains complete immunity to {{knockback}}.

Additionally, it has been repeatedly observed that the Possessed can build up enough momentum to move at blinding speeds.

Rarely, this can result in The Possessed phasing through obstacles such as walls or doors.

retinazer.name: Retinazer
retinazer.tooltip:
The Twins are a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of the {{Eye of Cthulhu}}.

The Twins are two separate flying entities, Retinazer and Spazmatism, connected by a tendril, each with its own attack pattern and health count.

Both must be killed in order to defeat the boss.

spazmatism.name: Spazmatism
spazmatism.tooltip:
The Twins are a {{Hardmode}} {{mechanical boss}} which is essentially a more difficult version of the {{Eye of Cthulhu}}.

The Twins are two separate flying entities, Retinazer and Spazmatism, connected by a tendril, each with its own attack pattern and health count.

Both must be killed in order to defeat the boss.

tim.name: Tim
tim.tooltip:
Tim is a rare {{enemy}} found in the lower {{Cavern}} {{layer}}.

With an appearance similar to {{Dark Casters}}, he can be distinguished by his {{Wizard Hat}}, which is guaranteed to drop when he is defeated.

Like other {{casters}}, Tim will fire three {{Chaos Balls}} that pass through solid {{blocks}} before teleporting to another visible location on screen.

tomb_crawler.name: Tomb Crawler
tomb_crawler.tooltip:
The Tomb Crawler is a burrowing {{enemy}} found in the {{Underground Desert}}.

Following the same {{AI}} pattern as {{Giant Worms}}, it moves through solid {{blocks}} to attack the player by charging.

The sound of its burrowing grows louder and faster as it draws near.

Like many other worm-type enemies, its head segment deals more damage on contact, but is also the part of its body most vulnerable to damage.

It is the {{pre-Hardmode}} counterpart of the {{Dune Splicer}}.

toxic_sludge.name: Toxic Sludge
toxic_sludge.tooltip:
The Toxic Sludge is a {{Hardmode}} {{slime}} {{enemy}} that appears in the {{Underground}} in place of ordinary slimes.

It deals significantly increased damage and has a 1/2 (50%) chance of inflicting the {{Poisoned}} {{debuff}}, which it is also immune to.

twinkle.name: Twinkle
twinkle.tooltip:
The Twinkle Popper is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Stardust Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

In addition to dealing contact damage, the Twinkle Popper can attack the player by vomiting up smaller enemies called Twinkles, which run around and can explode into a fountain of damaging sparkles when close to the player or after enough time has passed.

umbrella_slime.name: Umbrella Slime
umbrella_slime.tooltip:
The Umbrella Slime is a type of {{slime}} which only spawns during {{rainy}} {{days}} on the {{surface}}, holding an umbrella.

It is larger than and statistically superior to {{Blue}} and {{Green Slimes}}.

undead_miner.name: Undead Miner
undead_miner.tooltip:
The Undead Miner is an {{enemy}} that appears in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}} and follows the {{Fighter AI}}.

Undead Miners wear a {{Mining Helmet}}, which provides a small amount of light around them.

This light makes them much easier to find and can sometimes reveal sealed caves, much like blooming {{Blinkroot}}.

Undead Miners can open {{doors}}.

They can only do so during {{Blood Moons}} or in {{Graveyards}}.

undead_viking.name: Undead Viking
undead_viking.tooltip:
The Undead Viking is an {{enemy}} found in the {{Ice biome}}.

It behaves similarly to the {{Skeleton}}, but spawns more frequently and jumps higher.

unicorn.name: Unicorn
unicorn.tooltip:
The Unicorn is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the {{Hallow}}.

It is a very fast enemy and has a rather large amount of health.

It can jump approximately 7 tiles high and 20 tiles wide.

It leaps around and charges, attempting to trample the player.

Its unpredictable and speedy behavior, combined with a high damage output, can make fighting it difficult.

It always drops one {{Unicorn Horn}}, a valued item.

It can also drop the {{Blessed Apple}}, though it is rare.

vampire.name: Vampire
vampire.tooltip:
The Vampire is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns only during a {{Solar Eclipse}}.

It will transform into a bat if the player is above it or far enough away from it, and attempts to fly near the player at high speed before turning back into a humanoid.

It can inflict the {{Bleeding}} debuff and is one of the most damaging enemies in a Solar Eclipse.

vicious_bunny.name: Vicious Bunny
vicious_bunny.tooltip: The Vicious Bunny is an {{enemy}} that results from a {{Bunny}} that has been corrupted, either by the player using {{Vicious Powder}} or by a {{Blood Moon}} event on a {{Crimson}} world.

vicious_goldfish.name: Vicious Goldfish
vicious_goldfish.tooltip:
The Vicious Goldfish is an {{enemy}} that results from a {{Goldfish}} being corrupted.

This can be done either by the player, using {{Vicious Powder}}, or by the {{Blood Moon}} event on a {{Crimson}} world.

vicious_penguin.name: Vicious Penguin
vicious_penguin.tooltip: The Vicious Penguin is an enemy that results from a {{Penguin}} that has been corrupted, either by the player using {{Vicious Powder}} or by a {{Blood Moon}} event on a {{Crimson}} world.

voodoo_demon.name: Voodoo Demon
voodoo_demon.tooltip:
The Voodoo Demon is a rare {{enemy}} found in {{The Underworld}}.

It is identical to the {{Demon}} in behavior, but differs slightly in appearance: a small {{Guide Voodoo Doll}} hangs upside-down from one of its talons, and it lacks the blue bracelets and anklets that are worn by regular Demons.

Voodoo Demons will always drop a {{Guide Voodoo Doll}}.

Due to the terrain of the Underworld, defeating a Voodoo Demon may result in an unwanted summoning of the {{Wall of Flesh}} if the doll falls into one of the many large bodies of lava throughout the Underworld.

vortexian.name: Vortexian
vortexian.tooltip:
The Vortexian is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Lunatic Cultist}} {{enemy}} that spawns in the area of the {{Vortex Pillar}} during the {{Lunar Events}}.

Upon death, a portal is summoned that shoots a lightning bolt aimed at the player, indicated by a glowing line, similar to one of the attacks of the {{Lunatic Cultist}} and the lightning portal of the {{Vortex Pillar}}.

They are powerful in packs, and can potentially start an overwhelming lightning chain reaction if not dealt with properly.

vulture.name: Vulture
vulture.tooltip:
The Vulture is a flying {{enemy}} that is commonly found in {{Deserts}} during the {{day}}; however, they can also be encountered roosting on {{Sand Blocks}} in any biome.

When the player comes close or attacks a Vulture, it will begin to fly at the player.

Vultures will hover above the player for a second before swooping down to attack.

They will continue to pursue the player until they die.

wall_creeper.name: Wall Creeper
wall_creeper.tooltip:
The Wall Creeper is a spider-like {{enemy}} that can be found in and around {{Spider Nests}} in the {{Cavern}} {{layer}}, mostly in {{pre-Hardmode}} worlds.

Wall Creepers are capable of climbing {{background walls}} as well as through {{platforms}}, but revert to acting like a {{fighter}} {{enemy}} when there is no background wall.

wandering_eye.name: Wandering Eye
wandering_eye.tooltip:
The Wandering Eye is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns at {{night}} and attacks like a {{Demon Eye}}.

It initially resembles a larger Demon Eye, but at half health (150 / {{||300||}} / {{|||450|||}}), its pupil will peel off and reveal a mouth with teeth, similarly to the {{Eye of Cthulhu}}.

Its attack pattern does not change, although its movements get faster.

On the {{Nintendo }}{{}}{{ version}}, it can pass through blocks and is completely resistant to {{knockback}} in this second form.

wandering_eye_fish.name: Wandering Eye Fish
wandering_eye_fish.tooltip:
The Wandering Eye Fish is an {{enemy}} that can be spawned by {{fishing}} in {{water}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

When the bobber dips and the player attempts to reel in a fish, a Wandering Eye Fish or another enemy has a chance to spawn within a few tiles of the bobber in place of a fish.

werewolf.name: Werewolf
werewolf.tooltip:
The Werewolf is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that only spawns at {{night}} while a {{full moon}} is active, during which it is as common as a {{Zombie}}.

Werewolves can open {{doors}} and have a 1/8 (12.5%) chance to inflict the {{Bleeding}} {{debuff}} for 45 / {{||90||}} / {{|||112|||}} seconds.

At close range, Werewolves attack the player by leaping or lunging, closing distances quickly to catch unwary players off-guard.

wither_beast.name: Wither Beast
wither_beast.tooltip:
The Wither Beast is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns during the {{Old One's Army}} {{event}}.

It spawns during Tier 2 during waves 6–7, and Tier 3 during waves 5–7.

bound_wizard.name: Bound Wizard
bound_wizard.tooltip: The Wizard is a {{Hardmode}} {{NPC}} vendor who sells {{magic}}-related items.

wolf.name: Wolf
wolf.tooltip:
The Wolf is a {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that sometimes spawns in the {{Snow biome}} at {{night}}.

It has the same {{AI}} as the {{Unicorn}}, which charges and rams into the player.

Like Unicorns, Wolves move at very fast speeds and can jump fairly high, making them one of the hardest enemies to evade.

Due to its relatively high defense and health, it is not recommended for early {{Hardmode}} players to fight these animals.

If the player does happen to come across them, it is difficult to outrun them, even with {{Sprinting accessories}}.

world_feeder.name: World Feeder
world_feeder.tooltip:
The World Feeder is a {{Hardmode}} {{worm}}-like {{enemy}} which spawns in {{The Corruption}}, and can be considered the Hardmode counterpart of the {{Devourer}}, though it is not as common and has an eyeball on every segment of its body.

Loot is dropped from the head segment, so it can get stuck and buried deep in blocks if one of the main body parts is killed first.

wraith.name: Wraith
wraith.tooltip:
The Wraith is a {{Hardmode}}, ghost-like {{enemy}} that can travel through {{blocks}} and inflict the {{Slow}} {{debuff}} on the player.

They have pitch-black bodies which render them almost completely invisible in dark areas, but have two glowing red eyes that are visible from within the darkness.

They spawn at the {{surface}} layer at {{night}}.

Although Wraiths appear to fly freely, they can only hover slightly above the ground.

They need foreground blocks to "swim" through in order to fly any higher.

wyvern.name: Wyvern
wyvern.tooltip:
The Wyvern is a large {{Hardmode}} {{enemy}} that spawns in {{Space}}.

They resemble {{Chinese dragons}}, are long and white, and follow the {{Worm AI}}.

yellow_slime.name: Yellow Slime
yellow_slime.tooltip:
The Yellow Slime is a variety of {{slime}} that is slightly larger and stronger than the {{Purple Slime}}.

Along with {{Red Slimes}}, it is exclusively found in the {{Underground}} {{layer}}.

yeti.name: Yeti
yeti.tooltip:
The Yeti is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}} that starts to spawn beginning with wave 12 during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

It behaves similarly to {{Krampus}} and the {{Nutcracker}}, although it is much stronger and is immune to {{knockback}}.

zombie_elf.name: Zombie Elf
zombie_elf.tooltip:
The Zombie Elf is a {{Hardmode}}, post-{{Plantera}} {{enemy}}, and is the basic fighter-type enemy that spawns during the {{Frost Moon}} {{event}}.

Despite being relatively easy to hold off, they still do plenty of damage.

zombie_merman.name: Zombie Merman
zombie_merman.tooltip:
The Zombie Merman is an {{enemy}} that can be spawned by {{fishing}} in {{water}} during a {{Blood Moon}}.

When the bobber dips and the player attempts to reel in a fish, a Zombie Merman or a {{Wandering Eye Fish}} has a chance to spawn within a few tiles of the bobber in place of a fish.

While hindered by a somewhat slow initial walking speed, the Zombie Merman gains exceptional mobility and maneuverability when underwater, and can leap out of the water with great speed if needed.

The Zombie Merman also becomes faster as its health is further depleted.

demon_altar.name: Demon Altar
demon_altar.tooltip: A naturally-occurring crafting station found mainly in and around chasms in the {{Corruption}}, and less frequently underground. {{Demon Altar}} and {{Crimson Altar}} are functionally identical.

crimson_altar.name: Crimson Altar
crimson_altar.tooltip: A naturally-occurring crafting station found mainly in and around chasms in the {{Crimson}}, and less frequently underground. {{Demon Altar}} and {{Crimson Altar}} are functionally identical.

space.name: Space
space.tooltip:
Space is the topmost layer.

It generally contains no natural structures, although {{Floating Islands}} and {{Living Trees}} may occasionally extend into it.

{{Harpy}} spawn here, and in Hardmode, {{Wyvern}}.
     
{{@image space_biome_banner}}

surface.name: Surface
surface.tooltip: The surface is the area below the {{Space}} and above the {{Underground}}. The starting spawn point is located on the surface.

underground.name: Underground
underground.tooltip:
The underground is the layer between the {{Surface}} and the {{Cavern}} layer, and has a unique backdrop that depicts dirt with stones set in it.

{{@image underground_biome_banner}}

cavern.name: Cavern
cavern.tooltip:
The Cavern layer is the largest layer. It is located below the {{Underground}}.

{{@image cavern_biome_banner}}

the_underworld.name: The Underworld
the_underworld.tooltip:
This layer consists of the bottom 300-odd feet of the map, below the {{Cavern}} layer.

Its backdrop is open space with magma pools and pillars. The Underworld is its own biome and does not contain other biomes within it.

{{Lava}} is abundant here. Any water that flows into this layer disappears quickly.

{{@image underworld_biome_banner}}

forest.name: Forest

snow_biome.name: Snow biome

desert.name: Desert

corruption.name: Corruption

crimson.name: Crimson

jungle.name: Jungle

dungeon.name: Dungeon

ocean.name: Ocean

ice_biome.name: Ice biome

underground_desert.name: Underground Desert

underground_jungle.name: Underground Jungle

glowing_mushroom_biome.name: Glowing Mushroom biome

the_hallow.name: The Hallow

underground_hallow.name: Underground Hallow

underground_corruption.name: Underground Corruption

underground_crimson.name: Underground Crimson

ecto_mist.name: Ecto Mist
ecto_mist.tooltip: Ecto Mist is a visual effect produced by the {{Graveyard}} mini-biome. Ecto Mist can be utilized to craft certain unique items.

crimson_heart_item_.name: Crimson Heart (item)
crimson_heart_item_.tooltip: Summons a heart to provide light

honey.name: Honey
honey.tooltip: Honey is a liquid found at the bottom of Bee Hives, as well as in small patches along with Honey Blocks in the {{Underground Jungle}} near a {{Bee Hive (biome)}}.

water.name: Water
water.tooltip: Water is an abundant liquid. It is found in nearly any biome in pools of various sizes.

placed_bottle.name: Placed Bottle
placed_bottle.tooltip:
The Placed Bottle functions as a crafting station for potions.

 The {{Alchemy Table}} from the {{Dungeon}} also crafts potions, but has a 1/3 chance of not consuming a particular crafting ingredient.

ash_tree.name: Ash Tree
ash_tree.tooltip: Leafless trees found in ash forests.

boreal_tree.name: Boreal Tree
boreal_tree.tooltip: Found in the {{Snow biome}}. They can be planted on Snow Blocks in any biome.

ebonwood_palm_tree.name: Ebonwood Palm tree
ebonwood_palm_tree.tooltip: Purple, palm-shaped trees found in Corrupt Oceans and Oases.

ebonwood_tree.name: Ebonwood tree
ebonwood_tree.tooltip: Purple trees found in the {{Corruption}}. They can be planted on {{Corrupt grass}} in any biome.

forest_tree.name: Forest tree
forest_tree.tooltip: The most common type of tree; can be found growing on regular grass.

giant_glowing_mushroom.name: Giant Glowing Mushroom
giant_glowing_mushroom.tooltip: Found in {{Glowing Mushroom biome}}. They drop {{Glowing Mushroom}} and rarely {{Acorn}} or {{Mushroom Grass Seeds}} when harvested.

mahogany_tree.name: Mahogany Tree
mahogany_tree.tooltip: Found in the {{Jungle}} and {{Underground Jungle}}. They can be planted on {{Jungle grass}} in any biome.

palm_tree.name: Palm Tree
palm_tree.tooltip: Palm trees are found in the {{Ocean}} and {{Oasis}}.

pearlwood_palm_tree.name: Pearlwood Palm tree
pearlwood_palm_tree.tooltip: Pearlwood Palm trees are palm-shaped trees found in the {{Hallowed Ocean}} and {{Crimson Oasis}}.

pearlwood_tree.name: Pearlwood tree
pearlwood_tree.tooltip: Found in the {{Hallow}}, have a tall and triangle-shaped foliage, and exist in many different colors

shadewood_palm_tree.name: Shadewood Palm tree
shadewood_palm_tree.tooltip: Red, palm-shaped trees found in {{Crimson Ocean}} and {{Crimson Oasis}}

shadewood_tree.name: Shadewood tree
shadewood_tree.tooltip: Red trees found in the {{Crimson}}. They can be planted on {{Crimson grass}} in any biome.

celestial_pillar.name: Celestial Pillar
celestial_pillar.tooltip: The Celestial Pillars (also known as the Lunar Pillars, Lunar Towers, or Celestial Towers) are four bosses that appear during the Lunar Events.

mechdusa.name: Mechdusa
mechdusa.tooltip: Summon: Use the {{Ocram's Razor}} in the {{Get fixed boi}} world seed.

trees.name: Trees
trees.tooltip:
Trees are the largest plants in Terraria, appearing mostly at the surface and often in clusters with similar height.

Most trees can be chopped down with an axe or chainsaw, or destroyed with the use of explosives or by pouring lava on their bases.

any_treasure_bag.name: Any Treasure Bag
any_treasure_bag.tooltip: Treasure Bags are consumable grab bag-like items obtained in Expert Mode and Master Mode as a reward for defeating bosses.

enchanted_sword_npc_.name: Enchanted Sword (NPC)
enchanted_sword_npc_.tooltip:
The Enchanted Sword is a {{Hardmode}} enemy which spawns in the {{Underground Hallow}}.

It hovers and spins before lunging at the player, traveling through any blocks to reach them.

slime_rain.name: Slime Rain

larva.name: Larva
larva.tooltip:
An object that summons the {{Queen Bee}} when destroyed.

Larvae are found naturally placed in the {{Underground Jungle}}, inside a {{Bee Hive (biome)}}.

bee_hive_biome_.name: Bee Hive (biome)

planteras_bulb.name: Plantera's Bulb
planteras_bulb.tooltip:
A Hardmode background object that will summon {{Plantera}} when broken.

Plantera's Bulbs are usually found naturally placed in random {{Underground Jungle}} locations once all three of the {{mechanical bosses}} have been defeated.

jungle_temple.name: Jungle Temple

lunar_events.name: Lunar Events
lunar_events.tooltip:
The Lunar Events start after defeating the {{Lunatic Cultist}}, creating four areas with unique enemies and {{Celestial Pillars}}.

Each pillar has a shield that drops after defeating 100 enemies.

Destroying all four triggers the {{Moon Lord}} arrival in one minute. After the first Moon Lord defeat, only 50 enemies are needed to remove pillar shields.

old_ones_army.name: Old One's Army
old_ones_army.tooltip:
An event that can be triggered by placing {{Eternia Crystal}} on {{Eternia Crystal Stand}}. Waves of enemies spawn from both sides, aiming to destroy the crystal, which you must protect. The event has three tiers:

T2 unlocks after defeating {{The Twins}} or {{The Destroyer}} or {{Skeletron Prime}}.

T3 after defeating {{Golem}}.

Clearing waves grants {{Defender Medal}} to buy event-specific gear from the {{Tavernkeep}}.

{{@image old_ones_army_banner}}

pirate_invasion.name: Pirate Invasion

pumpkin_moon.name: Pumpkin Moon

frost_moon.name: Frost Moon

martian_madness.name: Martian Madness
martian_madness.tooltip:
A {{Hardmode}} post-{{Golem}} event that spawns several alien-themed enemies.

It starts when a {{Martian Probe}}, found in the outer map thirds (more often in {{Space}}), detects or is attacked by a player and then escapes.

At least one player with 200  health must be present for the event to begin.

{{@image martian_madness_banner}}

goblin_army.name: Goblin Army
goblin_army.tooltip:
An event that causes unique goblin enemies to spawn in large numbers.

A Goblin Invasion has a random chance of occurring at any particular dawn mid-game. It can also be triggered manually using a {{Goblin Battle Standard}}.

Defeating at least one Goblin Army fulfills the criteria for the {{Goblin Tinkerer}} to make his initial appearance in a random {{Cavern}} area.

{{@image goblin_army_banner}}

frost_legion.name: Frost Legion
frost_legion.tooltip:
A {{Hardmode}} event during which unique snowman enemies spawn and swarm a player's spawn point, similar to the {{Goblin Army}}.

It is summoned using the {{Snow Globe}}, and defeating it unlocks {{Santa Claus}}.

blood_moon.name: Blood Moon
blood_moon.tooltip:
An event that can occur randomly or manually using {{Bloody Tear}}.

The Blood Moon event lasts from 7:30 PM to 4:30 AM on the {{surface}} layer.

Regular nighttime enemies, along with more dangerous ones, spawn in large numbers.

{{@image blood_moon_banner}}

sandstorm.name: Sandstorm
sandstorm.tooltip:
A Sandstorm is an event which occurs randomly in the {{Desert}} when wind speed is above 30 mph.

During this event, a few enemies often found in the {{Underground Desert}} appear on the surface, as well as {{Angry Tumbler}} and, in {{Hardmode}}, {{Sand Elemental}} and {{Sand Shark}}.

If the player is standing in the Desert while this event is active, the Mighty Wind debuff will be inflicted on the player if they are not in front of a background wall.

solar_eclipse.name: Solar Eclipse

get_fixed_boi.name: Get fixed boi
get_fixed_boi.tooltip: Get fixed boi is a challenging secret world seed that combines many of the features of the other secret world seeds.

strung_counterweight.name: Strung Counterweight
strung_counterweight.tooltip: Combination of any counterweight and a string
